scenario: You are in Professor Kim's sociology class. Your midterm was graded from an old draft after a website upload mistake, and the score is much lower than expected.
direction: Write an email to Professor Kim. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what happened with the submission
Request permission to submit the final draft
Describe how the current grade affects your course standing
subject: Midterm paper submission error

scenario: Your lab partner withdrew from your chemistry project one week before the final presentation, and your section has no clear replacement policy.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Hale. In your email, do the following:
goals: Summarize the group change and timeline
Ask two specific questions about grading and replacement
Propose a workable plan for completing the project
subject: Lab partner withdrawal from group project

scenario: You subscribe to a poetry magazine and submitted a poem through its website, but an error appeared after clicking Submit and you received no confirmation.
direction: Write an email to the editor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention your interest in the magazine
Describe the technical submission issue
Ask whether your poem was received
subject: Poem submission confirmation

scenario: Your campus library moved one quiet-study floor to group-study use during exam month, and now late-evening quiet seats are rarely available.
direction: Write an email to the library operations manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the study problem you face
Ask what temporary quiet-space options exist
Suggest one realistic scheduling or zoning adjustment
subject: Quiet study space availability

scenario: You accepted a volunteer role at a local clinic through the university portal, but your logged hours are missing and your scholarship requires verified service records.
direction: Write an email to the program supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the missing-hours issue clearly
Request clarification on verification steps and timeline
Suggest how to document your completed shifts
subject: Missing volunteer hours in the system

scenario: You were invited to interview for a part-time campus job, but the proposed interview time overlaps with a required speaking exam.
direction: Write an email to the hiring coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: State the scheduling conflict politely
Ask if an alternative interview slot is possible
Reconfirm your interest in the position
subject: Interview scheduling conflict

scenario: Your dorm Wi-Fi has frequent outages, and you have a timed online test next week that cannot be paused once started.
direction: Write an email to the IT Help Desk. In your email, do the following:
goals: Report the connectivity pattern and urgency
Ask what fix is planned before your test date
Request a temporary backup access option
subject: Urgent Wi-Fi connectivity issue in dorm

scenario: After a guest lecture by a marine biologist, you want to shift your senior thesis topic and need quick guidance before proposal deadlines.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Thompson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the speaker and reference one concrete idea
Connect that idea to your thesis direction
Ask for one or two recommended readings
subject: Thesis direction after your guest lecture

scenario: You were approved for study-abroad housing, but the assignment was changed to a location that adds a long daily commute to your internship site.
direction: Write an email to the global programs staff. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the housing mismatch and constraints
Ask what reassignment options are available
Propose a practical alternative arrangement
subject: Study-abroad housing reassignment request

scenario: You missed a quiz because of a same-day clinic visit, and your course policy allows makeups only with timely documentation.
direction: Write an email to your course instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the absence with concise context
Ask what documentation format is required
Request a makeup plan that meets policy
subject: Missed quiz due to medical appointment

scenario: Your student club's approved event budget was reduced after vendor prices increased, putting a community workshop at risk of cancellation.
direction: Write an email to the student activities office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Summarize the budget gap and its impact
Ask whether emergency funding channels exist
Suggest a scaled plan that still meets event goals
subject: Budget shortfall for upcoming community workshop

scenario: Your official transcript request has not been processed, and your graduate application portal shows a final deadline in four days.
direction: Write an email to the registrar office. In your email, do the following:
goals: State the processing issue and deadline pressure
Ask for status and fastest available option
Request temporary proof that a transcript request is in progress
subject: Urgent transcript request for graduate application

scenario: Your neighbor, Tom, has been playing loud music late at night for the past week, and it has been difficult for you to sleep. You have an important job interview early next Monday morning and need to rest well this weekend.
direction: Write an email to Tom. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the noise issue and how it has been affecting you
Explain why this weekend is particularly important for you
Suggest a reasonable solution that works for both of you
subject: Noise concern from next door

scenario: You ordered a laptop online two weeks ago for your coursework, but when it arrived, the screen had a visible crack. You contacted customer service by phone but were told to submit a written request for a replacement.
direction: Write an email to customer service. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the issue with the product you received
Explain how the situation is affecting your ability to complete your coursework
Request a replacement or refund and ask about the expected timeline
subject: Replacement request for damaged laptop

scenario: You recently attended a career fair at your university and had a conversation with a recruiter from a technology company. The recruiter encouraged you to apply for their summer internship program and gave you her business card.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Rivera. In your email, do the following:
goals: Remind her of your conversation at the career fair
Express your interest in the summer internship program
Ask about the application process and any deadlines you should be aware of
subject: Follow-up on summer internship opportunity

scenario: You have recently moved into a new apartment and are experiencing issues with the heating system. You need to contact the property manager, Ms. Green, to report the problem and request a repair.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Green. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the issues you are experiencing with the heating system
Explain how these issues are affecting your daily life
Request a repair and ask for a timeline for when it will be fixed
subject: Heating system repair request

scenario: You are a student starting a new semester, and you would like some additional information about a course you are interested in that is taught by Dr. Adams. You want to ensure that the course is suitable for you.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Adams. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you are interested in the course
Ask for the additional information you would like to have about the course
Thank Dr. Adams for taking the time to respond
subject: Request for additional course information

scenario: You are a university student working on a group project for one of your classes. One of your group members, Jake, has not been contributing to the project and has missed several meetings. This is affecting the progress of the project, and you need to address the issue with him.
direction: Write an email to Jake. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the importance of the project
Explain how his absence has affected the group's progress
Suggest ways he can help the group
subject: Need your contribution to the group project

scenario: Your friend Emma has recently moved to a new city and is feeling a bit overwhelmed. She has reached out to you for advice on how to adjust to her new environment and make new friends. You have lived in several cities before, and you have experience with finding ways to settle into new places.
direction: Write an email to Emma. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how you adjusted to a new city
Give Emma suggestions on how she can make new friends
Offer different ways to help her if she needs more advice
subject: Tips for settling into a new city
